title: CCR Q3

title: CCR Q2

title: Film Screening
description: Today was the film screening event in our college where the projects of all the students were showed on an SMD screen in the Gym Hall. It was a great experience looking at the creative view and skills of all our piers. This event was a great addition to our term and everyone was really impressed as this was something implemented by our new media teacher who just joined the faculty this year. This was something new for us because it had never taken place in our college before.

title: Final Product
description: Luckily My "First Draft" is also the "Final product". I was one of only 3 students in or class who's First draft was passed by our media teacher as a Final product. Basically we three were the best filmmakers in class.

title: Premiere Pro Vs Davinci
description:
Now I am at the last stage of the production process and I have to make a choice between davinci resolve and Adobe Premiere Pro. I Have used a bit of both but I have more experience with Adobe so I think I'll be going with adobe.

The main reason for going with adobe is first of all my mindset is a bit straight forward I dont like investing in different things so basically if I learn two skills that clash together it kind of triggers me. This project will polish my Adobe Skills a lot and secondly the UI and Everything on Adobe is very easy to use compared to Davinci. shifting from any other software to Davinci straight up feels like switching from a an Automatic car to a Boeing 777.

title: BTS
description:
Today was shoot day and was Fun, hectic........ and a lot more stuff.

But in the end the shoot came out good and we had a great time. Something the four of us will never forget for sure.

title: Shoot Day
description: Tomorrow is shoot day I have done all the preparations all the gear is ready only the camera is being charged. The Caste is also ready for tomorrow but the only issue right now is that we don't have any spare days. Basically both the caste members Shawaiz and Ahmed are free tomorrow but not day after tomorrow so we have to perfectly execute the shoot tomorrow as we have no chances of scheduling a reshoot.

title: Location
description: For Location I have the best place in mind which is none other than my own House. This is because I have the perfect room behind my house that was supposed to be servant quarter but has been abandoned for a long while and has the best suspicious kind of look we are looking for. Secondly my house's driveway can be used for the shots showing the Characters leaving for the "Operation". Lastly to end the film opening and to show the start of the "Operation" we have selected a nearby petrol pump which has a parking lot behind it and a container that is being used by them as a storage room.

title: Mood Board
description: Visualizing the aesthetic and atmosphere of Operation Red & Black. This mood board captures the stark contrasts, cinematic lighting, and intense energy that define our thriller's world.

title: Inspiration
description:
We watched a lot of films, but very few aligned with the vision we had for our project. After watching John Wick, we all agreed that this was the one film that truly reflected our direction. It presents a world of highly skilled assassins operating within a structured, secret society, where rules, codes, and reputation define survival. The main focus is on John Wick, a retired hitman who is pulled back into the underworld after a personal loss, leading to a relentless pursuit of revenge. The film showcases intense, choreographed action sequences, close-quarters combat, and precise gunplay, all set within stylized environments like nightclubs, hotels, and city streets.

As the story unfolds, it becomes clear that this world is much larger than just one man’s mission. Organizations like the Continental Hotel act as neutral grounds, governed by strict rules, while powerful figures manipulate events from behind the scenes. The climax highlights not just physical combat but strategy, endurance, and control, as John outmaneuvers his enemies despite overwhelming odds. This inspired our film to focus on sleek action, disciplined combat, and a narrative driven by revenge, secrecy, and the hidden structures of power within a modern espionage world.

title: Main Idea
description: My Main Idea is to go for something inspired by the John wick and Mission Impossible series' because I always wanted to work on something similar to them plus I'm a big fan of Tom Cruise.

title: The Script
description:
Most of the script was written by me but the other videographer who will be helping be during the shoot was also involved a bit in script writing. He basically made some minor changes in the script after I was done writing it.

Our Media teacher has also approved the script so It's now time for our shoot.

title: The Caste
description: For the Caste I needed two people who could be shown as exact opposites. I chose two friends of mine one really tall and another pretty short. It will be pretty easy to show the contrast between both these actors, one will be wearing black clothing and the other will be wearing Red clothing. The roles of both are still to be decided according to available wardrobe.

title: Budgeting
description: the Expected budget for this film opening is not too high because I already have all the Equipment I will be using so there wont be any rental costs. The main costs will be the logistics and food for the actors and a secondary videographer who will be helping out in some shots.

title: Pre-Liminary Task 3
description: My Last Pre-Liminary task was a photography task was a photography task and I was supposed to capture nature specially because of the beautiful weather in our city recently. The submission was a bit late but I got some amazing pictures

title: Pre-Liminary Task 2
description:
For My second Pre-liminary Task I was assigned to make a short edit or highlight video of anything I am interested in.

I Have good connections in the Event Industry so I Decided to get a media pass for an upcoming event through a friend and I made a highlight reel of that event. The Organisers ended Up Liking the Reel so they even posted it on their socials.

title: Preleminary task 1
description:
For my preliminary task I chose to create a video on the famous Pakistan monument. It felt like the perfect subject, a blend of architecture and history.

My gear for this task was limited, so I used the DJI Air 2S. It was the only equipment I could source, and honestly, it proved to be a game-changer. The entire video was shot from an aerial perspective, giving a unique, almost cinematic feel to the monument. Initially, I considered using the drone as a ground-level camera to capture closer angles, but in a public space, it felt awkward. I couldn’t shake off the feeling that I looked like a maniac trying to maneuver a drone inches from the ground.

title: History of Films
description: The history of film started in the late 19th century when motion picture technology was invented. Pioneers in this were Thomas Edison and the Lumiere brothers as they were the ones who invented devices that could now take video, even though these were short and without any audio it was still a huge leap forward from just pictures. The real rise of films began in the 1940s because all the fun stuff was now here. By fun stuff i mean color films, special effects and new genres. By the 1980s the industry was huge.

title: Genre
description:
What is the meaning the word "Genre"?

Well according to google it means "a category of artistic composition, as in music or literature, categorized by similarities in form, style or subject matter". This explanation is first of all very difficult and second of all not specifically for films, So basically in easy words genre is a method of grouping similar film together. For example the genre "horror" includes films that have jump scares or other disturbing scenes. Different Genres can also be mixed to produce films for example "horror comedy"

Comedy: It is a genre that focuses on making the audience laugh by adding funny scenes to the film.

Mystery: It is a genre in which the main event is kept a mystery until the end of the film and in usually contains a "plot twist" as well.

Horror: It is a genre that intends to frighten or disturb the audience through jump scares or disturbing scenes.

Romance: It is a genre in which a love story is the main plot and usually closes with a happy ending.

Adventure: It is a genre where the protagonist goes on a long journey in search of something and encounters hurdles in the way.

Crime: It is a genre that involves major crimes such as murders or kidnappings and involves the story of the suspects, witnesses, Investigators and victims.

Action: It is a genre where the protagonist goes into a series of events that involve dynamic scenes such as fights, escape scenes or even high speed chases.

Sci-Fi: It is a genre in which the protagonist navigates futuristic worlds and advanced technology this genre often involves alien life, space travel or even time travel.

title: My Favourite TV Shows
description:
As i said earlier i really love watching Tv Shows and series, so these will have more love behind them compared to my favorite movies. The only beef i have with Tv shows is that they take a lot of time to watch, this is because i mostly try to binge watch because i always want to know what happens in the next episode.

Breaking Bad

This series is just a masterpiece and anyone who has watched it will agree with me. I mean the way Walter White turns from a boring old chemistry teacher to a drug lord is just phenomenal. You though i would miss out jessie pinkman? No! jessie was a very fun guy but i always ended up feeling very bad for him, there were just too many tragedies that happened to him.

Peaky Blinders

My favorite TV show of all time. I mean do i even have to say anything? the mis-en-scene was just top notch, the suits were what i loved the most. A honorable mention i would like to add here would be Luca Changretta i mean everyone loves Thomas Shelby and Cillian murphy but Changretta holds a special place in my heart. Adrien Brody really nailed this role.

Game Of Thrones

Game of thrones it would have been up there as my favorite series of all time replacing peaky blinders but the last season ruined it, anyone who has watched the series can understand me. Other then this the series is great, the story, the production, they nailed everything other then the last season. The character i loved most in the series was Arya stark because of the character development, she was the youngest stark and most of all in a misogynistic world and still she single-handedly avenged her father.

title: My Favourite Films
description:
I am not a huge fan of movies but i do love watching Series, don't ask me why cuz many people have asked me the same question "why do you like TV shows more then movies". I just don't have a valid reason. Might be because TV shows have a larger story and more character development?

Movies I like

The Old Guard

I really like the story of this film and I overall love the concept of immortality in this movie, it is shown as both a curse and a power which I love. The action scenes from this movie were also very good as they hook the viewers in and it almost felt like I was in the fights.

Joker

I recommend this one to literally anyone who asks me for a movie recommendation. Todd Philip’s direction became the 1st R rated movie to gross $1 Billion at the box office. The movie deals with the themes of isolation, existentialism, conformity and compulsion. Joaquin Phoenix who played Arthur Fleck bagged an Oscar for this memorable performance. The dark visuals of the 1980s Big Apple amplify every minute of this work of art.

The Godfather

A personal favorite, this cinematic masterpiece is one of the highest-rated movies on IMDb with a staggering rating of 9.2. the 20th-century Italian crime epic’s direction encapsulates the literary essence of the novel on the silver screen and Its cinematography visualized the depth of every scene to its potential.

IT (2017)

The summer holiday antics of seven socially awkward nerdy kids, this movie was my 1st experience of cosmic horror, the character of Pennywise. To me, in every scene where Pennywise is anticipated to appear was memorable yet frightening, his arbitrary expressions alone made this movie an easy top 10 on my list.

title: A Bit About Me
description: I have no real experience in filmmaking. I mean, I do know how to fly FPV drones, but that isn't useful for real production—or maybe it is. We'll see. I do have some experience with video editing, but that's about it. [Full text pending update]

title: Annural Khalid x Groovon - Music Video
description: A previous work collaboration featuring Annural Khalid. This project focused on smooth transitions and a sleek urban aesthetic, showcasing early forays into music video production and rhythmic editing.

title: Operation: Red and Black - The Script
description: The detailed production script for our cinematic film opening. Shot-by-shot breakdown focusing on precision, tension, and the 'Mission: Impossible' aesthetic.
content:
OPERATION: RED AND BLACK
Detailed Production Script – Cinematic Format
Runtime: 1:50
Format: Production-only (Shot-by-shot)
V.O. (Male) throughout, ending at title reveal.

DIRECTOR’S NOTE
This film opening should feel sleek, tense, and professional — minimal dialogue, heavy atmosphere.
Lighting emphasizes contrast: red and black hues dominate.
Camera movement is smooth and deliberate — no handheld chaos.
The pacing builds from calm precision to controlled intensity.
Music: low synths, ticking rhythm, crescendo near the title.

SCENE 1 – “THE SUIT-UP” (00:00–00:35)
Location: Dimly lit garage or storeroom
Lighting: One RGB panel light (warm tone), low-intensity fill from LED stick (side angle).
Sound: Low hum of synth; faint metallic clicks.
SHOT 1A (00:00–00:05) – EXTREME CLOSE-UP (ECU)
Black leather gloves tighten. Soft light flickers across his hands. Camera locked on tripod — steady frame.
V.O. (Male): “You’re not gonna find anyone better…”

SHOT 1B (00:05–00:10) – CLOSE-UP (CU)
Mask slides over Red’s face. Light shifts slightly red from LED stick. Slow DOLLY-IN using gimbal.

SHOT 1C (00:10–00:15) – MEDIUM SHOT (MS)
Both figures — Red & Black — zip duffel bags. Camera pans left as they turn simultaneously.
Music pulse begins (bass layer).
V.O. (Male): “These two—they’re ghosts. Red and Black.”

SHOT 1D (00:15–00:25) – INSERT / DETAIL SHOTS
• Gloved hands loading pistols and tools.
• Close-up of watch syncing (beep).
• LED flicker timed to music beat.
Camera: Handheld gimbal, slow motion. Lighting: Red bounce fill from panel light.

SHOT 1E (00:25–00:35) – WIDE SHOT (WS)
Silhouettes of both walking toward a car. Camera TRACKS BACK slowly; drone hovers above for aerial top-down pullout.
V.O. (Male): “Once they’re in, there’s no trace left behind.”
Cut to black. Low bass hit transitions into engine rev.

SCENE 2 – “THE DRIVE” (00:35–01:15)
Location: Empty street / parking lot at night.
Lighting: Natural streetlight + blue stick light inside car.
Sound: Engine idle, muffled synth beats.
SHOT 2A (00:35–00:45) – INT. CAR – CLOSE-UP (CU)
Red’s gloved hand grips steering wheel. Camera mounted on dashboard using tripod. Focus shift to side mirror reflection of Black loading weapon.
V.O. (Male): “They work clean. Precise.”

SHOT 2B (00:45–00:55) – WIDE SHOT (WS – DRONE)
Drone follows the car exiting garage in slow motion. Tilt-up to reveal night skyline. LED taillights glow red against black asphalt.

SHOT 2C (00:55–01:05) – MEDIUM SHOT (MS – MOVING)
Mounted gimbal shot from passenger seat. Camera pans to Black — calm, silent — checking scanner device. Red nods once.
V.O. (Male): “Never loud. Never messy.”

SHOT 2D (01:05–01:15) – AERIAL TRACKING (DRONE)
Overhead drone shot follows car turning into industrial area. Cross-dissolve to next location. Bass builds.

SCENE 3 – “THE TARGET ZONE” (01:15–01:45)
Location: Industrial site / warehouse exterior
Lighting: Stick light (red hue) hidden behind props for edge glow.
Sound: Tension pulse, scanner beeps, low wind.
SHOT 3A (01:15–01:25) – WIDE SHOT (WS)
Both figures exit car. Camera TRACKS them from behind at waist level. Fog or dust in background for depth.
V.O. (Male): “You see… when they move, it’s like clockwork.”

SHOT 3B (01:25–01:35) – CLOSE-UP (CU)
Black kneels, activates handheld scanner. Light reflects off mask. Camera DOLLYS-IN slowly from right. Beeping increases.

SHOT 3C (01:35–01:45) – DRONE OVERHEAD / HIGH ANGLE
Red scans the perimeter. Both freeze as device pings green. Camera rises dramatically as the signal peaks.
V.O. (Male): "No hesitation. No noise. No mercy."

SCENE 4 – "TITLE REVEAL" (01:45–01:50)
SHOT 4A (01:45–01:48) – CLOSE-UP
Scanner flashes green. Cut to black instantly.
SHOT 4B (01:48–01:50) – TITLE CARD
Bold red and black gradient appears center frame. Bass hit echoes and fades.
V.O. (Male): "They call it... Operation: Red and Black."
